name: Dahi
subtitle: The original Indian live culture ferment
blrNote: Bengaluru's warmth is ideal - set it in the evening, have fresh dahi by morning. Use full-fat milk from any dairy or Nandini.

IN.where: Any dairy, Nandini booths, or supermarket
Any kirana or use your last batch

equipment.name: Wide ceramic or steel container
equipment.notes: For setting the dahi - wide surface = faster setting

tldr: Heat milk, cool to warm, stir in a spoon of last batch, leave overnight. Wake up to dahi. Repeat forever.

steps.title: Heat the milk
steps.instruction: Bring milk to a full boil, stirring occasionally. This kills competing bacteria and partially denatures proteins for a thicker set.
tips: Don't skip boiling - it's what makes Indian dahi different from Western yogurt
Stir constantly near the bottom to prevent a skin from forming and milk from scorching
Let the milk foam up and settle 2–3 times for an even thicker, creamier set
steps.checkpoint: Milk has come to a rolling boil

steps.title: Cool to the right temperature
steps.instruction: Let milk cool to 40–45°C. The old test: dip your clean finger - it should feel warm but not burn. If you can hold your finger comfortably for 5 seconds, it's right.
tips: Too hot kills the culture. Too cold and it won't set.
In BLR, this usually takes 35–40 min at room temp
Transfer to a wide steel vessel to cool faster - a narrow pot traps heat and takes much longer
steps.checkpoint: Milk feels comfortably warm on your wrist

steps.title: Add starter
steps.instruction: Put your 2 tbsp of dahi into the setting vessel. Add a small amount of warm milk and whisk smooth, then pour in the rest of the milk. Stir gently.
tips: Mix starter smooth first to avoid lumps in the final dahi
A thick, sour starter from a neighbour's kitchen will give better results than watery store-bought curd
Use a ceramic or earthen pot (handi) for setting - the porous surface retains warmth and improves texture

steps.title: Set overnight
steps.instruction: Cover loosely. Leave undisturbed in a warm spot. In Bengaluru's climate, room temperature is sufficient - no need to wrap in towels. Check after 6–8 hours.
tips: Don't move or jostle the container while it's setting
In cooler BLR winter months (Dec–Jan), place near the stove or in a closed cupboard
Wrapping the vessel in a thick towel or placing it inside the oven (turned off) holds warmth all night
steps.checkpoint: Dahi has set - tilting the container shows a solid mass

steps.title: Refrigerate
steps.instruction: Once set, refrigerate. It will firm up further and sourness will develop slowly over the next day.
tips: Save 2 tbsp from this batch as your next starter
Refrigerate within an hour of setting - leaving it out longer makes it noticeably more sour
If you want thick Greek-style dahi, hang it in muslin for 2 hours and collect the whey for chapati dough

images.caption: Dahi
images.attribution: Photo by Sara Cervera on Unsplash
images.alt: Dahi - The original Indian live culture ferment

culturalContext.story: Dahi has been made in the Indian subcontinent for at least 5000 years. It appears in the Rigveda, in Ayurvedic texts, in every regional cuisine. It is simultaneously medicine, comfort food, religious offering, and daily nutrition. Each household maintains a living culture passed through generations - sometimes decades old.
culturalContext.historicalNote: The practice of setting dahi is considered sacred in many households. The culture is treated as a living thing - it is "fed" new milk and ideally never allowed to die out. Some families maintain the same culture for generations.
culturalContext.significance: In Bengaluru, dahi (mosaru in Kannada) is foundational. Curd rice (mosaranna) is the ultimate comfort meal. Majjige (spiced buttermilk) is the summer survival drink. Raita accompanies almost every meal. Making your own dahi means you understand the live culture principle that underlies all dairy fermentation.
culturalContext.funFact: The word "dahi" likely derives from Sanskrit "dadhi," one of the five products of the cow considered sacred in Hindu texts. The other four: milk, ghee, butter, and cow urine.

thingsToAccountFor.title: Culture quality degrades
thingsToAccountFor.description: If you've been using the same starter for many generations without refreshing from a new source, the culture weakens. If dahi stops setting properly, start fresh from a good store-bought yogurt.

thingsToAccountFor.title: Summer sourness
thingsToAccountFor.description: In hot weather, dahi sets faster and becomes more sour. Check earlier (4–5 hours) and refrigerate promptly.

thingsToAccountFor.title: Watery layer on top
thingsToAccountFor.description: Whey separation is normal and healthy - it means fermentation worked. Stir it back in or drain it off (whey is nutritious, use in cooking).

dehydratorIntegration.method: Spread strained dahi (labneh-style) thinly on parchment. Dehydrate at 45°C for 10–14 hours.
dehydratorIntegration.result: Yogurt powder - adds probiotics and tang to smoothies, raitas, or dry rubs.
tips: Strain whey first for faster dehydration
Add salt + herbs before dehydrating for flavoured yogurt chips

variations.name: Shrikhand
variations.description: Hang dahi in muslin cloth overnight, then whip with sugar, cardamom, and saffron. Maharashtrian/Gujarati festive dessert.

variations.name: Labneh
variations.description: Same strained hung curd concept, but finish with olive oil and zaatar. Middle Eastern pantry staple.

variations.name: Coconut milk dahi
variations.description: Replace dairy with full-fat coconut milk (Maggi or homemade). Vegan, works with same culture. Slightly looser set.
